extra.message: User-supplied 'name' parameter is injected into SQL via Python string formatting ('%(name)s') instead of parameterized query. An attacker can craft a name value to execute arbitrary SQL. The input originates from POST /students/ in sqli/views.py:57.

POST handler for /students/ lacks any authentication check. Anyone can create students by sending a POST request, even though the template conditionally hides the form from unauthenticated users. No @authorize() decorator or manual auth check is present.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: POST handler for /courses/ lacks any authentication check. Anyone can create courses by sending a POST request, even though the template only shows the form to admin users. No @authorize() decorator or manual auth check is present.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: POST handler for /courses/{course_id}/review lacks any authentication check. Anyone can create course reviews by sending a POST request. No @authorize() decorator or manual auth check is present.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: POST handler for /students/{student_id}/evaluate/{course_id} lacks any authentication or admin authorization check. Anyone can evaluate students, even though the template only shows the evaluate form to admin users. No @authorize() decorator is used.

cwe: CWE-306: Missing Authentication for Critical Function

extra.message: Jinja2 autoescaping is explicitly disabled (autoescape=False). Multiple templates render user-supplied data without the |e filter: course.jinja2 renders course.title (line 14) and course.description (line 15) without escaping, and review.review_text (line 22) without escaping; students.jinja2 renders student name (line 16) without escaping. Stored XSS is possible by injecting HTML/JS into course titles, descriptions, reviews, or student names.

CSRF middleware is commented out (csrf_middleware). All POST endpoints are unprotected against CSRF attacks even though CSRF tokens are generated in templates via csrf_processor. An attacker can forge state-changing requests on behalf of authenticated users.

Password verification uses unsalted MD5 hashing (md5(password.encode('utf-8')).hexdigest()). MD5 is cryptographically broken for password hashing and no salt is used, making stored hashes vulnerable to rainbow table and brute-force attacks.

cwe: CWE-327: Use of a Broken or Risky Cryptographic Algorithm

extra.message: Session cookie is configured with httponly=False. This allows JavaScript to access the session cookie, making session hijacking possible if XSS is exploited. Combined with disabled autoescaping and missing CSRF protection, this significantly increases the impact of any XSS vulnerability.

Application runs with debug=True, which may expose detailed error traces and internal application state in production. This can leak sensitive information about the application architecture, file paths, and database structure.

The 50x error template renders raw exception internals via {{ error.__dict__ }}, exposing internal application state including file paths, database connection details, and stack trace information to end users. Combined with debug=True, this leaks significant internal details.

cwe: CWE-209: Generation of Error Message Containing Sensitive Information

extra.message: Database credentials (username: postgres, password: postgres) are hardcoded in the configuration file committed to the repository. These credentials are used in production-like Docker Compose setup.
